mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month growth rate in nominal retail sales signals strengthening consumer demand and upward earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment crosses above a severe-stress threshold, household income risk and credit losses rise, causing equity investors to mark down future earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional returns at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the policy rate is pinned near zero while unemployment is high, monetary easing dominates labor weakness by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-low and UNRATE-high regime has nonpositive average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if its returns are not higher than high-UNRATE periods without low FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: If consumer prices accelerate while the federal funds rate remains near zero, expected real rates fall but inflation uncertainty and margin pressure dominate, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month growth crosses above its 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or if CPI acceleration conditional on low FEDFUNDS has no negative return coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in industrial production below a contraction threshold signals weak factory output and falling operating leverage, leading analysts to cut cyclical earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO-below-threshold triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the triggered mean return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts cross below a depressed activity threshold, residential investment and related durable-goods demand weaken, lowering expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-700 triggers have average 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than unconditional returns, or if the negative coefficient is unstable across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A higher 1-month change in payroll employment signals improving labor income and near-term revenue momentum, prompting positive equity repricing and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS changes fail to predict higher 5-day S&P 500 returns than negative PAYEMS changes, or if walk-forward hit rate remains below 52% after transaction-date alignment.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth exceeds CPI growth, real consumption momentum improves rather than reflecting only price increases, raising expected revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses above CPIAUCSL 3-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if real retail momentum triggers do not produce positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if RSAFS growth loses predictive power after conditioning on CPIAUCSL growth.
